title: The Wyvern's Terms
description: A wyvern has claimed the mountain pass and the kingdom's trade road lies silent. You carry a diplomat's seal and orders to negotiate, not fight.
author: AI

intro.passage: You stand at the foot of Ashridge Peak where the wyvern Skorrath has claimed the mountain pass. The kingdom's trade road lies silent, choked with fear and scattered bones. You carry a diplomat's seal, a waterskin, and orders to reopen the route by any means short of war.

ridge-path.passage: The trail switchbacks up the mountainside through loose shale and wind-bent scrub. Halfway up you find a narrow ledge overlooking the pass below. Scorch marks blacken the stone and the air tastes of sulfur. A cave mouth opens to your left, breathing warm air that carries a faint animal musk.

hermit-hut.passage: A crooked hut sits in the shadow of a great boulder, its chimney trailing wisps of smoke. Inside, an old woman stirs a clay pot that smells of iron and sage. She studies your diplomat's seal and nods. She knew the wyvern before it claimed this pass, and offers a charm to help.

bone-pass.passage: The trade road narrows between cliff walls scarred by claws ten feet above your head. Ox bones and shattered wagon wheels crunch beneath your boots. A low rumble echoes from deeper in the pass and shadows shift on the ledges above. Something very large knows you are here.

side-cave.passage: The cave opens into a vaulted chamber lit by thin cracks in the ceiling. Old straw and shed scales the size of dinner plates cover the floor. In the far corner sits a clutch of three pale eggs, warm to the touch and faintly pulsing. A scraping sound echoes outside.

summit-overlook.passage: From the summit you see everything. Skorrath's roost crowns the opposite peak, a nest of timber and rusted armor. The pass below is a chokepoint. A crude signal mirror left by a previous scout glints among the rocks. The wyvern circles above the far ridge, wings spread wide.

charm-accepted.passage: The hermit places a flat river stone in your palm. Spiraling runes cover its surface and it hums faintly against your skin. She warns it will let you speak with Skorrath but offers no protection from fire or fang. She tucks a bundle of dried silverleaf into your pack as well.

skorrath-history.passage: The hermit tells you Skorrath was driven from the eastern mountains by dragon hunters ten years ago. The wyvern is not cruel but guards this pass because it is the last territory left to it. Her voice carries old grief. She offers a charm that may help your cause.

parley-ground.passage: Skorrath lands before you with a thunderous crack of leathery wings. Dust and pebbles fly in all directions. The wyvern is enormous, scarred along its flanks, and ancient. One amber eye fixes on you. The beast does not attack. It lowers its head and waits, nostrils working your scent.

scattered-cargo.passage: Merchant goods lie scattered across the road. Bolts of dyed silk, cracked spice jars, a locked iron strongbox half buried in dust. A leather-bound journal lies open nearby, its pages turning in the wind. The last entry is dated three weeks ago. Two things here could change the negotiation.

egg-guardian.passage: You plant yourself between the eggs and the cave mouth as a shadow fills the entrance. Skorrath's massive head appears, nostrils trailing smoke. The wyvern sees you near its young and a terrible rumble builds in its chest. Then it notices your open hands. The rumble fades to silence.

roost-approach.passage: You pick your way down the far slope to Skorrath's roost. Up close the nest is enormous, built from whole tree trunks, wagon wheels, and plates of rusted armor. The wyvern is away. Among the debris you spot a small carved wooden figure placed with obvious care atop a flat stone.

charmed-pass.passage: The charm hums against your chest as you enter the bone-strewn pass. When Skorrath appears the wyvern pauses mid-stride, nostrils flaring at the silverleaf scent. The runes on your stone glow faint blue. A deep vibration forms words inside your mind. You carry the mark of Maren, it says.

seal-presented.passage: You hold up the diplomat's seal. Skorrath sniffs the metal and snorts a puff of acrid smoke. The wyvern understands kingdoms well enough but a seal is just a piece of metal. It scrapes one massive claw across the stone, drawing a line between you and it. Prove your intent.

tribute-offer.passage: You describe the kingdom's offer. Fifty head of cattle delivered each season to the base of the pass in exchange for safe passage along the road. Skorrath listens with one eye half closed. The wyvern is tired of hunting scattered mountain goats. But its pride runs deep. It scratches a counter-offer.

silent-stand.passage: Minutes pass in the howling wind. Skorrath studies you with ancient patience and you do not flinch. The wyvern lowers its scarred head until one amber eye is level with your own. You see yourself reflected in that golden depth, small but unwavering. Skorrath exhales a long warm breath across your face.

journal-evidence.passage: The journal tells a clear story. Every caravan attack was provoked by guards who loosed arrows first. Skorrath never struck an unarmed traveler or a wagon that kept its distance. The wyvern defends its home but does not hunt people. This changes the shape of the negotiation entirely.

strongbox-letter.passage: The lock yields to a sharp stone. Inside you find gold coins and a sealed letter between two merchant lords. The letter proposes hiring dragon hunters to kill Skorrath and splitting the salvaged trade profits. The wyvern is not the real threat here. Human greed is.

nest-trust.passage: Skorrath withdraws just enough for you to breathe. The terrible rumble in its chest softens to something almost like a question. Your steady hands and honest face have earned a fragile moment of trust. The wyvern looks from you to the eggs and back. It is waiting for you to speak.

maren-figure.passage: The figure is old oak worn smooth by years of handling. It depicts a woman holding a walking staff. At the base, carved in tiny letters, you read one name: Maren. This was placed with care, not hoarded like treasure. Skorrath values something beyond gold here. Someone it remembers.

maren-memory.passage: Skorrath's mind-voice grows quiet and tender. Maren was the hermit when she was young and walked the high passes without fear. She found the wyvern wounded and near death after the dragon hunters struck. She healed it over many weeks. They were companions once. Your charm proves you come in peace.

pact-negotiation.passage: You and Skorrath negotiate through the charm's bond, images and feelings flowing between your minds. The wyvern wants safety for its young, freedom to hunt the high peaks, and silverleaf each autumn. You offer the kingdom's guarantee. Each concession is weighed with care. The terms take shape between you.

respect-earned.passage: Skorrath pulls back and dips its massive head. The gesture is unmistakable, a greeting between equals. You have earned something rare through patience alone. The wyvern settles onto its haunches and the tension leaves the air. Whatever you propose next, this creature will hear it with an open mind.

hermit-return.passage: You carry the carved figure back down to the hermit's hut. The old woman takes it in both hands and her eyes fill with tears. She carved it forty years ago, she says. She gathers her walking staff and cloak. Together you climb toward the pass. She has words for an old friend.

egg-thief-ending.passage: You wrap one of the pale eggs in your cloak and turn to leave. The cave shakes. Skorrath fills the entrance with a roar that cracks stone from the ceiling. Fire blooms between its jaws. There is no bargaining with a parent whose child you hold. The flames come fast.

ending.title: Ashes and Folly
ending.flavor: Some things were never yours to take.

trade-ending.passage: The terms are struck. Cattle and safe passage in exchange for the wyvern's protection of the road from bandits and rockfall. You carve your acceptance into the stone beside Skorrath's claw mark. The wyvern watches with one half-lidded eye. By morning the first wagons will roll through the pass again.

ending.title: Commerce and Claws
ending.flavor: The best deals leave both sides grumbling.

overreach-ending.passage: You push for better terms. Skorrath's eyes narrow and smoke curls thick from its nostrils. The wyvern has offered fair terms and you have insulted its patience. It rises to its full height, blocking the sky. The negotiation is over. You walk back down the mountain with nothing but your life.

ending.title: The Diplomat's Retreat
ending.flavor: Know when the deal on the table is the best one you will get.

wyvern-terms.passage: Skorrath breathes a ring of pale blue flame around you both, an ancient pact-mark that binds the agreement. The pass will open. The nest will be protected. Silverleaf and game will come each autumn. As you descend the mountain the charm grows warm against your chest. Behind you, Skorrath sleeps.

ending.title: The Maren Accord
ending.flavor: The oldest alliances are built on trust, not steel.

guardian-ending.passage: Skorrath agrees to open the pass on one condition. The kingdom must declare the nesting grounds protected territory. No hunter, soldier, or merchant may approach within a league of the eggs. In return the wyvern will guard the trade road. You carry this treaty home, its weight heavier than any sword.

ending.title: Warden of the Pass
ending.flavor: The strongest treaties protect what matters most.

conspiracy-ending.passage: You ride hard for the capital with the letter in hand. The evidence is clear. Two merchant lords are arrested before the week ends and their scheme dies in the light. When the first safe caravan rolls through the pass, you see Skorrath watching from the cliffs above with something like satisfaction.

ending.title: Follow the Gold
ending.flavor: The real monster had no wings at all.
